text: MarketReel — Global Film Distribution Intelligence AustraliaFilm Censorship & Certification GuidelinesAustralian Classification Board (ACB) — Regulatory Intelligence1. Regulatory AuthorityThe Australian Classification Board (ACB) is an independent statutory body classifying films under the Classification (Publications, Films and Computer Games) Act 1995. Classifications are legally binding for theatrical exhibition, physical media sales, and digital streaming. A three-member panel reviews each film. The Classification Review Board handles appeals. Online content falls additionally under ACMA jurisdiction via the Online Safety Act 2021.2. Classification SystemClassificationAudienceDescriptionAgent Decision RuleGGeneralSuitable for all agesFull release, all audiences, unrestricted marketing.PGParental GuidanceParental guidance recommended for under-15 (advisory only)Family release. Mild thematic advisory. No enforcement of age restriction.MMatureNot recommended for under-15 (advisory only, not enforced)Recommended adult supervision under-15. Strong content present but no access restriction.MA 15Mature Accompanied 15Restricted to 15; under-15 must be accompanied by parent or guardian in cinemaAge-restricted. Under-15s cannot attend unaccompanied. Age advisory mandatory in marketing.R 18Restricted 18Restricted to adults 18 and over; ID requiredAdult content. Age verification at cinema. Restricted advertising channels.X 18Restricted 18 explicitExplicitly sexual content for adults; only legal in ACT and NTOnly viable in two Australian territories. Not viable for national theatrical release.RC — Refused ClassificationBannedCannot be legally sold, exhibited, hired, or importedDo not release in Australia. Criminal liability for distributor.3. Refused Classification — Absolute ProhibitionChild sexual abuse material — absolute prohibition with criminal penalties.Detailed instruction in crime or violence that could endanger the public — e.g., operational bomb-making or weapons construction depicted approvingly.Detailed instruction in suicide methods — specifically prohibited under ACB guidelines.Content that promotes, incites, or instructs in matters of crime or violence.Bestiality — any sexual content involving animals.Content that offends against community standards of morality, decency, and propriety in such an extreme way that a reasonable adult would find it unacceptable — the 'community standards' test at its most restrictive.4. Content Thresholds by ClassificationContent CategoryMA 15 ThresholdR 18 ThresholdRC Risk ThresholdViolenceStrong action violence; moderate blood; limited injury dwellingStrong graphic violence; sustained brutality; significant goreInstructional violence; torture presented approvingly without narrative consequenceSexual contentBrief non-graphic nudity in sexual contextExplicit sexual content; nudity in sexual context throughoutHardcore explicit sex (X18 in ACT/NT only); bestialityLanguageVery strong or extensive strong languageAll language permissibleNo threshold for language aloneDrug useDrug use depicted; not heavily instructionalDrug use and abuse; more explicit depictionInstructional drug content; very detailed manufacture instructionHorrorStrong sustained horror; intense threat; some goreExtreme body horror; torture; very high gore volumesTorture presented approvingly; extreme real-seeming violence without redemptive contextSuicideDepicted without method detail; emotional portrayalGraphic or detailed depiction without clinical framingDetailed step-by-step method instruction5. LGBTQ ContentAustralia imposes no special restrictions on LGBTQ content. Classification is determined entirely by sexual content level and not by sexual orientation. Same-sex romance is classified identically to opposite-sex romance at all rating levels. Australia is one of the most permissive markets in the Asia-Pacific for LGBTQ content.6. Online Content — ACMA RulesContent hosted or streamed in Australia is subject to Australian classification standards under the Online Safety Act 2021.RC-classified content is illegal to host or stream in Australia — platforms can be required to block or remove content.R18 content online requires robust age verification systems.Major streaming platforms (Netflix, Disney, Stan, Binge) are required to apply equivalent classification standards to their Australian libraries.7. Classification Application Process & TimelineApply to the Australian Classification Board at www.classification.gov.au with required materials and fee.Panel of 3 classifiers views the film and determines classification.Decision issued with classification certificate.If classification unsatisfactory, apply to Classification Review Board within 30 days.Further appeal to Administrative Appeals Tribunal on legal grounds only.AGENT TIMELINE RULEStandard classification decisions take 2–4 weeks. Allow 6 weeks minimum from submission to confirmed classification before announcing Australia theatrical release date.8. Criminal liability for distributor.Instructional violence or terrorism detailRC riskRemove instructional elements. Narrative violence: retain. Practical instruction: remove.Explicit sexual contentR18 or X18R18 for mainstream theatrical. X18 restricted to ACT and NT only.LGBTQ contentLOWNo restriction. Rate based on sexual content level only.Strong violence or goreMA15 or R18Assess volume and intensity. Consider trim for MA15 if targeting broader audience.Drug use glamourisedMA15–R18Consequence framing recommended. Instructional content: RC risk.Strong language (pervasive)MA15Assess frequency and severity. Heavy profanity throughout: MA15.Horror — extremeR18Standard horror: MA15. Torture or extreme gore: R18.Suicide method depicted in detailRC riskRemove step-by-step method depiction. Emotional portrayal without method: MA15.MarketReel Confidential Australia Censorship Guidelines For Agent Reasoning & Internal Distribution Use Only
